findings.message: PII (owner email address) is written to application logs. The model definition explicitly annotates owner_email as PII with the instruction 'do not include in logs or external responses', and this log call violates that contract directly. Emails in logs propagate to log aggregation/retention systems with weaker access controls and indefinite retention.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from the log event. Log only non-PII identifiers (e.g. item_id). If an audit trail must associate the creation with an owner, log a non-reversible reference (hashed/tokenized id) or store the association in the access-controlled DB rather than the log stream.

findings.message: owner_email PII is collected via the ItemCreate request body but is never persisted for the stated purpose: create_item(session, payload.name) only passes name, so owner_email is accepted purely to be logged. Collecting PII that is not used for the operation's purpose (and only ends up in logs) is unnecessary collection.
findings.suggestion: Either drop owner_email from ItemCreate entirely if the create operation does not need it, or — if owner association is genuinely required — pass it to create_item so it is stored in the access-controlled owner_email column instead of being collected only to emit it to logs.
